title: Plumbers and heating and ventilating installers and repairers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Repair or remove and replace system components, including pipes, fixtures, and plumbing systems, ensuring functionality and compliance with standards.
Install plumbing components such as pipes, fittings, valves, and fixtures according to manufacturer specifications and building codes.
Connect water heaters and storage tanks to power and water sources, ensuring proper integration with existing plumbing systems.
Fill tanks and check pipes and fixtures for leaks, using appropriate testing methods to ensure system integrity and safety.
Assess collector sites and determine locations for installing solar subsystem components, ensuring optimal performance and safety.
Install solar thermal systems, including collectors, controllers, and sensors, ensuring adherence to manuals and specifications.
Conduct routine maintenance or repairs for solar thermal systems to restore them to baseline operating conditions.
Install heat exchangers, circulating pumps, and pipe assemblies using hand tools, soldering equipment, and following schematics.
Inspect, examine, or test installed plumbing and mechanical systems using pressure gauges and other methods to ensure compliance and functionality.
Train or supervise others in the installation and maintenance of plumbing systems to ensure safe and effective practices.
Maintain or repair plumbing by replacing defective components, mending broken pipes, or addressing clogs as needed.
Design plumbing system layouts, including pipe sizes and types, following specifications and codes for efficient installation.
Evaluate plumbing work sites for hazards and obstructions, coordinating installations around existing systems and structures.
Write reports or keep records of work assignments and maintenance activities for plumbing projects and systems.
Install automatic controls and monitoring system components to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of plumbing systems.
Participate in project planning, including estimating costs and coordinating with other trades to ensure seamless installation processes.
Repair, adjust, or replace electrical or mechanical components or parts, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering or welding equipment.
Complete repair bills, shop records, time cards, or expense reports.
Disassemble machines to examine parts for wear or defects, using hand or power tools and measuring devices.
Clean, oil, or adjust mechanical parts to maintain machines' operating efficiency and prevent breakdowns.
Enter information into computers to copy programs or to draw, modify, or store schematics.
Maintain parts inventories and order any additional parts needed for repairs.
Analyze equipment performance records to assess functioning and diagnose malfunctions using testing devices.
Inspect and test electrical systems and equipment to locate and diagnose malfunctions, using visual inspections and testing devices.
Train new repairers and provide technical customer service.
Maintain equipment logs that record performance problems, repairs, calibrations, or tests.
Consult with customers and supervise or coordinate efforts for equipment installation or maintenance.
Install and configure new equipment, including operating software or peripheral equipment.
Reassemble machines after making repairs or replacing parts; test and verify functionality.
Diagnose problems with electronic equipment, and replace or repair defective components, wiring, and relays.
Run signal quality and connectivity tests for cables and electrical connections, and record results.
Prepare reports and maintain records detailing tests, repairs, maintenance, and costs of repairs.
Conduct scheduled preventive maintenance tasks to detect and prevent equipment problems.
Study blueprints, schematics, manuals, and other specifications to determine installation procedures or equipment modifications.
Measure, cut, and install frameworks and conduit to support wiring and electrical systems, using hand tools.
Discuss plans with clients and contractors to ensure necessary changes and compliance with specifications.
Maintain contact with contractors and clients, formulating reports to ensure completion of work at minimum cost.
Requisition materials and supplies, such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts, needed for repair work.
Inspect and monitor work areas, tools, and equipment, providing employee safety training to prevent unsafe conditions.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using tools, hydraulic equipment, or welding equipment.
Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair, maintenance techniques, or equipment use.
Examine systems or facilities, analyzing information to determine needed installations, services, or repairs.
Investigate accidents or injuries, preparing reports of findings to ensure safety compliance.
Develop or implement maintenance policies and procedures to ensure efficient operation.
Compute estimates and actual costs of materials, labor, and outside contractors for repair work.
Review, evaluate, and coordinate completion of work bids from contractors to ensure project efficiency.

emerging_tasks.task: Perform installation, testing, adjustment, and repair of avionics equipment in uncrewed aerial vehicles, such as drones.
Develop plans for integration of drone technology into transportation systems for purposes such as delivery of goods or traffic monitoring.
Use drone technology for site surveying, inspection, and monitoring of infrastructure projects.
Use drones for inspection of high-voltage lines and other hard-to-reach equipment.
Communicate results in peer-reviewed research articles or at workshops or conferences.
Develop and test autonomous systems for uncrewed aerospace vehicles.
Develop plans for safely drydocking vessels, including structural integrity, block loading, and stability.
Investigate equipment failures or diagnose faulty operations and recommend or perform remedial actions.
Use agricultural drones for crop monitoring, irrigation management, and pest control.
